metric_value: Clear leader in high-end Web conferencing
notes: Aberdeen assessment as of April 2002; emerged as clear leader in past year.

metric_value: 5,000+ customers
notes: More than 5,000 customers as of April 2002 announcement.

metric_value: 800+ customers per quarter (virus-like, word-of-mouth-induced)
notes: Described as virus-like word-of-mouth growth; 40% of new customers referred by existing 5,000-customer base.

metric_value: ~40 companies competing to deliver shared PowerPoint presentations over Internet in 1999
notes: In 1999 WebEx lumped with ~40 competitors; by 2002 WebEx had pulled away as leader.

metric_value: Private conferencing switched network on top of Internet; utility-grade QoS and security
notes: Most significant differentiator is under-the-covers private WAN; competition has not built equivalent.

metric_value: 5 new capabilities: UCF, multi-document sharing, Access Anywhere, multipoint video, PDA support
notes: Third-generation WebEx infrastructure; Aberdeen research suggests impossible for older database-centric store-and-forward vendors to replicate.

metric_value: Dramatically enhances WebEx competitive position; most critical/demanded capability by end-users
notes: Aberdeen research: PowerPoint full animation over Web is single biggest competitive differentiator; prior DHTML attempts failed.

metric_value: Clear trend away from face-to-face meetings, especially large internal meetings and training
notes: 2001 recession and post-9/11 travel uncertainty accelerated shift; first time socially acceptable to not conduct all meetings face-to-face.

metric_value: Noun becoming verb: 'Let's WebEx'
notes: Aberdeen overheard the ultimate compliment of acceptance in market; 40% of new customers from word-of-mouth referrals.

metric_value: Full training schedule/registration; breakout sessions; testing/scoring; SCORM support; re-broadcast
notes: New service rolling out April 2002; existing customers already believe WebEx excellent vehicle for training.

metric_value: Face-to-face > Telephone > Audio Conference > Video Conference > Web Conference (hierarchy)
notes: Aberdeen model for real-time business communications with pros/cons for each level; WebEx positioned as enhanced 6th-level above standard Web conference.

metric_value: Fixed location; high capital cost; B2B video over Internet blocked by firewall security; requires ISDN lines
notes: Business-to-business video conferencing over Internet technologically feasible but firewall security forces use of multiple ISDN lines.

metric_value: WebEx provides utility-grade QoS; Internet provides none; competitors' erratic response times can be catastrophic
notes: Internet = 'World Wide Wait'; dropped audio/video frames or ill-timed page changes catastrophic to meeting goal; only WebEx resolves this.

metric_value: Media-rich: software demos, training, project management, staff meetings, remote support, sales/marketing presentations, seminars, partner briefings, customer service
notes: McLuhan: 'The media is the message'; WebEx shines in media-rich environments; adequate but not differentiated for content-lean meetings.

metric_value: Positioned to become dominant online meeting standard; making online meetings as straightforward as telephone calls
notes: Aberdeen conclusion: WebEx's head-turning formula; fast-growth high-tech companies already heavy users; shift to Web conferences is tectonic.

metric_value: Acquired by Cisco Systems in 2007 for approximately $3.2 billion; market leadership validated
notes: Paired outcome for OBS-015: Cisco WebEx acquisition validated Aberdeen's market leadership assessment; now Webex by Cisco.

metric_value: Database-centric store-and-forward approach led to many failures; cannot match Mediatone capabilities
notes: Aberdeen: software-driven store-and-forward approach led to many failures; architecturally limited vs WebEx's switched infrastructure.

metric_value: Internet VoIP suffers all security and privacy flaws of Internet; biggest Web conferencing drawback
notes: VoIP over Internet: inability to ensure consistent response times and service levels; WebEx private WAN resolves this.

metric_value: Very easy business case; presenters spend more time at jobs, less on airplanes; training has longer shelf life via recording/rebroadcast
notes: Training delivered simultaneously to large audiences; just-in-time delivery; Aberdeen expects Training Center to draw many new customers.

metric_value: Extremely unpredictable; significant limitations; not adopted by market
notes: DHTML approach to web presentation failed; WebEx UCF is the breakthrough solution delivering full PowerPoint animation.

metric_value: Power of multipoint video conferencing with just browser + webcam; also supports Polycam cameras and video camcorders
notes: Mediatone enables multipoint video without fixed video conferencing equipment investment.

metric_value: 2001 recession + post-9/11 travel uncertainty = tectonic market-shifting earthquake away from face-to-face meetings
notes: For first time socially acceptable to not conduct all meetings face-to-face; fundamental shift unleashed by recession and security concerns.
